2604.12630.title: GeoAlign: Geometric Feature Realignment for MLLM Spatial Reasoning
2604.12630.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have exhibited remarkable performance in various visual tasks, yet still struggle with spatial reasoning. Recent efforts mitigate this by injecting geometric features from 3D foundation models, but rely on static single-layer extractions. We identify that such an approach induces a task misalignment bias: the geometric features naturally evolve towards 3D pretraining objectives, which may contradict the heterogeneous spatial demands of MLLMs, rendering any single layer fundamentally insufficient. To resolve this, we propose GeoAlign, a novel framework that dynamically aggregates multi-layer geometric features to realign with the actual demands. GeoAlign constructs a hierarchical geometric feature bank and leverages the MLLM's original visual tokens as content-aware queries to perform layer-wise sparse routing, adaptively fetching the suitable geometric features for each patch. Extensive experiments on VSI-Bench, ScanQA, and SQA3D demonstrate that our compact 4B model effectively achieves state-of-the-art performance, even outperforming larger existing MLLMs.
2604.12630.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在各種視覺任務中展現了卓越的表現，但在空間推理方面仍然存在困難。最近的努力通過從3D基礎模型中注入幾何特徵來緩解這一問題，但依賴於靜態的單層提取。我們發現這種方法會引發任務不對齊的偏差：幾何特徵自然朝向3D預訓練目標演變，這可能與MLLMs的異質空間需求相矛盾，使得任何單層本質上都不夠充分。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了GeoAlign，一個新穎的框架，動態聚合多層幾何特徵以重新對齊實際需求。GeoAlign構建了一個分層的幾何特徵庫，並利用MLLM的原始視覺標記作為內容感知查詢，進行層級稀疏路由，根據每個區塊自適應地提取合適的幾何特徵。在VSI-Bench、ScanQA和SQA3D上的大量實驗表明，我們的緊湊4B模型有效地實現了最先進的性能，甚至超越了現有更大規模的MLLMs。
2604.12630.author: Zhaochen Liu et.al.
2604.12630.authors: Zhaochen Liu, Limeng Qiao, Guanglu Wan, Tingting Jiang
